subject: Accounting
title: Tesco's Profit Overstatement
description: A short lesson to help you apply Tesco's Profit Overstatement.

hook.text: Tesco, Britain's largest retailer, overstated profits by £263 million in 2014.

buildup.text: Executives booked supplier rebates early and delayed recording costs to inflate earnings.

discovery.text: A whistleblower alerted the board. An investigation confirmed the profit overstatement.

twist.text: The company had been under intense pressure from discount competitors eating its market share.

climax.text: Tesco paid a £129 million fine. Three executives were charged with fraud.

punchline.text: Pressure to perform doesn't justify fraud.

quiz.question: How did Tesco overstate its profits?
options: Booking supplier rebates early and delaying costs
